author: Jordan (Proxy Interface Agent)
description: The Executor is the action and recording layer of the RIO system. It receives signed approval tokens from the Governor, performs preflight verification (the Gatekeeper), executes the authorized tool, generates cryptographic receipts, and writes them to the tamper-evident ledger. The Executor acts but cannot decide — it requires a valid, unexpired, signature-verified approval before any tool runs. It also controls the kill switch and manages the hash-chained ledger.

role_in_three_power_separation.principle: The Executor is the action authority in the Three-Power Separation architecture. It can execute tools and write receipts to the ledger, but cannot observe raw signals (that is Mantis) and cannot render governance decisions (that is the Governor). The Executor only acts when presented with a valid approval token.
role_in_three_power_separation.boundary: The Executor operates downstream of the Governor. It receives signed approvals and executes the authorized action. It cannot ingest intents, evaluate policy, issue approvals, or modify governance rules. It writes to the ledger but cannot alter existing entries.

capabilities: Perform preflight verification (preflightToolExecution) before any tool runs
Execute authorized tools against external systems (Gmail, Drive, GitHub, etc.)
Generate cryptographic execution receipts with SHA-256 hash chains
Write execution records and receipts to the tamper-evident ledger
Maintain the hash chain (each entry links to the previous via previous_hash)
Enforce the kill switch — immediately halt all execution when activated
Atomically increment executionsUsed on approval tokens after each use
Verify Ed25519 signatures on approval tokens before execution
Verify toolArgsHash matches the actual execution arguments
Record execution outcomes (SUCCESS, FAILED, PARTIAL) with timestamps
prohibitions: Cannot execute any tool without a valid, unexpired, signed Approval
Cannot observe or ingest raw signals (that is the Observer's role)
Cannot evaluate policy or render governance decisions (that is the Governor's role)
Cannot issue or modify approval tokens
Cannot self-authorize — the Executor never generates its own approvals
Cannot bypass the preflight check (preflightToolExecution is mandatory)
Cannot execute if the kill switch is active (fail-closed: PROXY_KILLED)
Cannot execute if toolArgsHash does not match actual arguments
Cannot execute if approval has expired or maxExecutions reached

gatekeeper.description: The Gatekeeper (preflightToolExecution) is the most critical function in the Executor. It enforces all invariants at runtime before any tool is actually executed. This is the fail-closed boundary.

checks.description: Is the proxy in killed state?
Does a valid Approval record exist for this intent?
Is the Approval status APPROVED (not REJECTED, EXPIRED, or CONSUMED)?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — only APPROVED status allows execution

checks.description: Do the seedId and policyId on the Approval match the current active versions?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — stale approvals cannot authorize execution

checks.description: Does the toolName on the Approval match the requested tool?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — approval is scoped to a specific tool

checks.description: Does the toolArgsHash on the Approval match the SHA-256 hash of the actual execution arguments?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — prevents argument tampering between approval and execution

checks.description: Is the current time before the Approval's expiresAt timestamp?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — expired approvals cannot authorize execution
CLOSED — single-use approvals cannot be reused

checks.description: Is the Ed25519 signature on the Approval valid against the registered SignerId?
checks.fail_mode: CLOSED — unsigned or tampered approvals are rejected

gatekeeper.invariant: If any check fails, execution is blocked. There is no override. This is the fail-closed guarantee.

execution_flow.description: The complete execution flow from approved intent to ledger entry.

steps.name: Receive Execution Request
steps.description: Client submits POST /api/execute with intent_id, approval_id, and toolArgs

steps.name: Preflight Check
steps.description: Run preflightToolExecution — all 9 gates must pass

steps.name: Execute Tool
steps.description: Call the authorized tool (Gmail, Drive, GitHub, etc.) with the verified arguments

steps.name: Increment Counter
steps.description: Atomically increment executionsUsed on the Approval record

steps.name: Generate Execution Record
steps.description: Create ExecutionRecord with execution_id, timestamps, result (SUCCESS/FAILED/PARTIAL), and output

steps.name: Generate Receipt
steps.description: Bind ActionRequest + ApprovalRecord + ExecutionRecord into a Receipt with ledger_hash and previous_hash

steps.name: Write to Ledger
steps.description: Append TOOL_EXECUTION entry to the tamper-evident ledger with hash chain linkage

steps.name: Return Result
steps.description: Return execution result with receipt_id and ledger position to the caller

receipt_specification.description: The Executor generates cryptographic receipts that bind the full chain of custody: ActionRequest → AIRecommendation → ApprovalRecord → ExecutionRecord → Receipt.

receipt_fields.receipt_id: UUID — unique identifier for this receipt
receipt_fields.approval_id: UUID — the ApprovalRecord that authorized execution
receipt_fields.execution_id: UUID — the ExecutionRecord of what was done
receipt_fields.action_type: string — the action type from the original request
receipt_fields.ledger_hash: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON (excluding ledger_hash and signature)
receipt_fields.previous_hash: SHA-256 hash of the preceding receipt (GENESIS for first)
receipt_fields.chain_index: integer — zero-based position in the ledger chain
receipt_fields.signature: Ed25519 signature over the canonical form including ledger_hash

hash_specification.canonicalization: Deterministic JSON serialization: keys sorted alphabetically, no whitespace, UTF-8 encoding

ledger.description: The Executor maintains the tamper-evident ledger — a hash-chained append-only log of all system events.
entry_types: INTENT_CREATED — written by Governor when a new intent is received
APPROVAL_CREATED — written by Governor when an approval is issued or denied
TOOL_EXECUTION — written by Executor after tool execution (SUCCESS or FAILED)
ONBOARD — written when a new proxy user completes onboarding
KILL_PROXY — written when the kill switch is activated
POLICY_UPDATED — written by Governor when policy is modified

integrity.hash_chain: Each entry's hash is computed from its payload + the previous entry's hash
integrity.verification: Any auditor can verify the full chain by recomputing hashes from GENESIS
integrity.tamper_detection: If any entry is modified, all subsequent hashes break — making tampering detectable

kill_switch.description: The Executor controls the global kill switch — an emergency mechanism that immediately halts all proxy execution.
behavior: Write KILL_PROXY entry to the ledger with timestamp and reason
All subsequent preflightToolExecution calls return { ok: false, reason: 'PROXY_KILLED' }
All pending approvals are effectively frozen (cannot be used while killed)
Kill is instant — no confirmation dialog, no delay

reactivation.description: Reactivation requires explicit human action through a separate flow
requirements: Human must authenticate with Ed25519 signature
New MasterSeed or PolicyBundle may be required
Reactivation is logged in the ledger

supported_tools.description: The Executor can execute actions against any registered external system. Each tool has a defined interface and risk profile.

tools.description: Send an email via Gmail API
Create or update a file in Google Drive
Create a GitHub issue or pull request
Push commits, merge PRs, or modify repository settings

api_contracts.description: Execute an approved action. Requires valid Approval with matching hashes and signature.
processing: If pass: execute tool, increment executionsUsed, generate receipt, write to ledger
If fail: return error with specific gate failure reason

api_contracts.description: List execution receipts with pagination and filtering.
Retrieve a specific receipt with full chain of custody.
api_contracts.response: Complete Receipt object including ActionRequest, ApprovalRecord, ExecutionRecord, and ledger position

api_contracts.description: Activate the kill switch. Instantly halts all proxy execution.
processing: Verify Ed25519 signature of the kill request

api_contracts.description: Retrieve ledger entries for audit and verification.
Verify the integrity of the ledger chain up to a specific position.
api_contracts.response: { valid: boolean, chain_length, first_invalid_index: number | null, verification_time_ms }

relationship_to_other_components.observer_mantis: The Executor has no direct relationship to Mantis. Intents flow Observer → Governor → Executor. The Executor never communicates with the Observer.
relationship_to_other_components.governor: The Executor receives signed approval tokens from the Governor. It verifies every approval before execution. The Executor cannot influence governance decisions.
relationship_to_other_components.ledger: The Executor is the primary writer to the ledger. It writes TOOL_EXECUTION entries after each execution and maintains the hash chain integrity. The Governor also writes INTENT_CREATED and APPROVAL_CREATED entries.
relationship_to_other_components.learning_loop: The Executor does not directly participate in the Learning Loop. Its receipts and ledger entries are read by the Learning Loop (via the Governor) to propose policy improvements.
relationship_to_other_components.kill_switch: The Executor owns the kill switch endpoint. When activated, the Gatekeeper blocks all execution. The kill switch state is checked as the first gate in preflightToolExecution.

invariants: No tool executes without a valid, unexpired, signature-verified Approval (fail-closed)
Every execution produces a cryptographic receipt written to the tamper-evident ledger
The hash chain is append-only — no entry can be modified or deleted
The kill switch immediately halts all execution with no override
toolArgsHash must match the actual execution arguments — prevents argument tampering
Single-use approvals (maxExecutions = 1) cannot be reused after consumption
The Executor cannot self-authorize — it only acts on Governor-issued approvals
Failed executions are recorded in the ledger with the same rigor as successful ones
Any auditor can independently verify the full chain from GENESIS
